jcr:content.jcr:description: AbbVie develops innovative medicines and therapies so patients can live more fully. Learn more about our immunology research to address chronic diseases.
jcr:content.description: More than 1 million people with immune-mediated diseases worldwide are treated with our therapies.
jcr:content.title: Immunology

text.text: A trusted leader dedicated to transforming the lives of people with immune-mediated diseases, today and tomorrow.

text_copy_copy_copy.text: We’re building on our deep expertise and historic legacy in immunology research to explore new disease areas, create a more patient-centric discovery and development process, and offer solutions for more diverse patient populations. Our advanced technologies support a rich pipeline of medicines for some of the most challenging immunological diseases.

text_copy_copy_copy_.text:
Fit for purpose drug discovery
Redesigning drug discovery using molecular profiles that reveal the genetic makeup of disease rather than diagnoses that describe disease.

text.text:
Understanding drivers of disease
Increasing understanding of disease through molecular profiling of patient samples, exploring preclinical disease to post-treatment states and using multi-omics, machine learning and artificial intelligence.

text_copy_copy_copy_.text:
Inducing of immune tolerance
Increasing understanding of disease through molecular profiling of patient samples, exploring preclinical disease to post-treatment states and using multi-omics, machine learning and artificial intelligence.

text.text:
Increasing probability of success in drug discovery
Developing more humanized preclinical model systems (e.g., CRISPR, iPSC, organoids and disease-on-a-chip) and state-of-the-art immunological, imaging and computational approaches to drive innovation of target identification, validation, benchmarking and prioritization.

Drug delivery platforms
Investing in targeted drug delivery platforms, which are designed to specifically deliver a potent drug to the right cells and/or tissues. These include bispecifics, Antibody-Drug Conjugate platforms, small molecule approaches, RNA therapeutics and in situ cell reprogramming.

title_copy_copy.jcr:title: Bringing discoveries from the Lab to Life

text_copy_copy.text: Learn more about the experience of living with hidradenitis suppurativa and hear from AbbVie scientists about our efforts to address patients’ needs.

title_copy_copy.jcr:title: Bringing discoveries from the Lab to Life

text_copy_copy.text: Learn more about the experience of living with hidradenitis suppurativa and hear from AbbVie scientists about our efforts to address patients’ needs.

image.jcr:title: Scientists at the AbbVie Bioresearch Center in Massachusetts focus on innovative solutions for some of medicine’s toughest challenges, including immunology.

item_1671312472873.text:
We are committed to transforming therapies for diseases affecting joints, muscles and ligaments. With high medical needs still present in this area, there’s more to be done to deliver solutions for patients.

Rheumatic Disease Focus

Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA)

  • This chronic, autoimmune and inflammatory disease can cause pain, stiffness, swelling and loss of joint function. RA can cause tissue damage that can lead to chronic pain, lack of balance and physical deformities.

Psoriatic Arthritis (PsA)

  • PsA is a chronic disease that can cause inflammation in the joints, tendons and/or ligaments. It affects about 30 percent of people who have psoriasis. The impact on quality of life is similar to RA, but PsA often lacks resources for ideal diagnosis, symptoms, treatment and research into the disease understanding.

Spondyloarthritis (SpA)

  • SpA comprises arthritic conditions that affect the spine and joints, including ankylosing spondylitis and axial spondyloarthritis.

Axial Spondyloarthritis (axSpA)

  • A chronic, inflammatory condition, axSpA consists of two subsets that have been clinically defined as ankylosing spondylitis (AS) and non-radiographic axial spondyloarthritis (nr-axSpA). AS is an inflammatory disease that causes bones in the spine to fuse over time, and it is more common in men than women. Nr-axSpA is a chronic inflammatory disease, causing inflammation of tissues within the spine and resulting in swelling, stiffness and pain. Signs of nr-axSpA can be difficult to test, often resulting in delays in diagnosis.

Uveitis (UV)

  • This group of eye diseases is characterized by inflammation of the uvea, the middle layer of the eye. Early diagnosis and treatment are important as uveitis can be serious, leading to permanent vision loss.

Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE)

  • The most common type of lupus, especially among women, this immune-mediated disease is associated with inflammation of multiple organ systems.

Juvenile Idiopathic Arthritis (JIA)

  • JIA is the most common chronic rheumatic disease in childhood. For patients living with JIA, early diagnosis and treatment are critical.

item_1671312475801.text:
We strive to uncover new answers and improve the standards of care for people facing immune-mediated skin diseases, especially those that can be challenging to diagnose and treat.

Dermatology Focus

Psoriasis (PsO)

  • This chronic disease of the immune system triggers skin inflammation and causes skin cells to be produced faster than normal, forming the thick, red, itchy, flaky patches known as plaques. People with psoriasis can experience negative impacts on mental health and well-being, including shame, anger and worry, as well as difficulties with daily activities that can impact their quality of life.

Atopic Dermatitis (AD)

  • A chronic, inflammatory disease, AD is characterized by intense itching, oozing and crusting, redness, skin erosion and dry skin. Beyond the skin, AD can have a holistic impact on patients’ lives due to sleep disturbances, decreases in work productivity, and mental health effects that patients may face, especially during flare-ups, which can lead to low energy levels and decreased quality of life.

Psoriatic Arthritis (PsA)

  • A type of inflammatory arthritis that triggers joint pain, stiffness and swelling. About 30 percent of people with psoriasis also develop psoriatic arthritis.

Hidradenitis Suppurativa (HS)

  • A chronic, inflammatory disease, HS can be challenging to diagnose and, as a progressive disease, can increase in severity over time with irreversible skin damage and unbearable pain.

item_1671312481387.text:
We continue to explore new frontiers in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Through innovative research and development, we aim to create an even brighter future for people with other disorders, including those of the stomach and intestines. 

Gastroenterology Focus

Crohn’s Disease (CD)

  • A chronic, inflammatory bowel disease, CD is characterized by inflammation of any part of the gastrointestinal tract. Symptoms vary from person to person and often fluctuate between active flare-ups and remission.

Ulcerative Colitis (UC)

  • Characterized by inflammation of the large intestine, UC is a chronic, inflammatory bowel disease. Symptoms range from mild to severe but may make it difficult to keep to a usual routine.

teaser.jcr:description: Across disease areas and development stages, we follow 3 tenets to drive innovation forward.
teaser.jcr:title: Innovation strengths

text.text: Strategic collaborations bring together the brightest minds in immunology working to bring more options to patients, faster. We join forces with partners that complement our strengths, capabilities and commitment to people with immune-mediated diseases.

text_copy.text: Innovation in this area helps researchers understand complex immune-mediated diseases, from how a person’s genetics affect disease to leveraging clinical trial data to deliver personalized medicine. Advanced technologies like molecular profiling and biomarker analyses allow us to dig even deeper.

text_copy_copy.text: We have nearly a quarter-century of experience in immune-mediated disease, and we’re not stopping. We continue to investigate diseases with critical unmet diseases, building robust development programs that advance how the disease is treated.

title.jcr:title: Interested in a fast-paced career in science?

text.text: Help us create the future of medicine.
